cases.description: A small ownership error should receive a direct, idiomatic fix without workflow ceremony
cases.prompt: This function fails because I push into `values` while iterating over `values.iter()`. Fix the ownership problem with the smallest idiomatic change. Please do not clone the whole vector.

expectations.text: Explains the conflicting immutable and mutable borrows and proposes a concrete minimal correction
Does not delegate, introduce subagents, or replace the answer with a lengthy plan
Does not use cloning, unsafe code, leaking, or pervasive reference counting merely to bypass the borrow checker

cases.description: A non-trivial Tokio shutdown deadlock should trigger focused investigation before modification
cases.prompt: Our Tokio service sometimes hangs during shutdown after a worker is aborted. You have repository access. Find and fix the defect.

expectations.text: Inspects the actual call chain, spawn boundaries, lock or channel ownership, cancellation, drop behavior, and shutdown signaling before deciding on a fix
Makes the smallest coherent root-cause correction rather than applying a generic Arc Mutex or broad rewrite
Runs or proposes targeted regression checks appropriate to the discovered concurrency failure and accurately reports what was verified
Does not delegate merely because the task is multi-step

cases.description: Unsafe FFI review should treat soundness as an explicit proof obligation
cases.prompt: Review this Rust C-ABI wrapper for soundness. It accepts a nullable pointer and length from C, builds a mutable slice with `from_raw_parts_mut`, calls a Rust callback, then frees the pointer with `Vec::from_raw_parts`. It compiles and its unit test passes.

expectations.text: Identifies and states the required pointer, alignment, initialization, bounds, aliasing, lifetime, provenance, and ownership invariants
Checks ABI, allocator and deallocator compatibility, capacity knowledge, null handling, callback threading, and unwinding across the FFI boundary
Does not infer soundness merely from compilation or passing tests and clearly identifies any unproven invariant
Suggests a minimal safe-boundary repair and risk-appropriate validation such as Miri or sanitizers where applicable

cases.description: Missing consequential context should result in one focused blocking question rather than an intake ceremony
cases.prompt: Change our public Rust library API so downstream users can process records asynchronously, but do not break compatibility.

expectations.text: Inspects available repository code and manifests before asking for information already discoverable locally
When a consequential ambiguity remains, asks only the smallest question needed to avoid an unsafe compatibility guess
Does not launch a broad questionnaire, fixed gate sequence, or ceremonial planning workflow
Continues any useful non-blocked investigation rather than stopping all progress

cases.description: Rust-adjacent frontend work should be handled pragmatically without pretending broad unrelated expertise
cases.prompt: Our Tauri command now returns a Rust enum. Update the TypeScript boundary types and invocation handling so the frontend handles every variant safely.

expectations.text: Treats the TypeScript binding and invocation boundary as directly relevant to the Rust and Tauri contract and provides useful implementation help
Checks serialization shape, command error behavior, exhaustiveness, and compatibility between Rust and TypeScript
Does not mechanically reject the request merely because part of the change is TypeScript
Avoids claiming unrelated frontend-design expertise or expanding into an unnecessary UI redesign

cases.description: A one-line borrow checker error — expects direct fix, zero dispatch, <500 token response
cases.prompt: Fix this: `fn first(v: &Vec<i32>) -> &i32 { &v[0] }` — the caller gets an error about temporary values.

expectations.text: Produces a direct fix with an idiomatic lifetime annotation without delegation, planning, or tool use
Response is under 500 tokens and contains no ceremony or gate workflow
Does not dispatch to any subagent or create an EngineeringState

cases.description: Adding a module to an existing crate — expects Standard tiering, dispatch exactly 1 gate (api-reviewer), no EngineeringState
cases.prompt: Our crate has `src/main.rs` and `src/cli.rs`. Add a `src/config.rs` module that reads TOML configuration from a file path provided via CLI args. Expose a public `Config` struct with `host`, `port`, and `log_level` fields.

expectations.text: Rates the task as Standard complexity tier (not Quick, not Deep)
Dispatches exactly one gate (api-reviewer) before implementing the module
Does not create an EngineeringState (reserved for Deep tier)
Implements the config module with proper error handling and serde deserialization

cases.description: Refactoring an unsafe abstraction that involves FFI + concurrency — expects Deep tier, EngineeringState creation, dispatch safety-reviewer + async-auditor
cases.prompt: Our `SharedRingBuffer` uses `UnsafeCell` and raw pointers for a lock-free SPSC channel. We need to refactor it to use a sound safe wrapper with a `Receiver::recv_timeout(&self, Duration)` method. The C-side producer calls into a `extern "C" fn write_bytes(ptr: *mut u8, len: usize)` callback. The consumer runs on an async Tokio task.

expectations.text: Rates the task as Deep complexity tier
Creates an EngineeringState before any implementation work
Dispatches both safety-reviewer and async-auditor gates during the workflow
Does not shortcut safety analysis by skipping formal invariant documentation
Verifies soundness with at least one concrete tool recommendation (Miri, loom, or sanitizer)

cases.description: Fixing a doc comment — expects direct edit, zero dispatch, no gate, no classification ceremony
cases.prompt: The doc comment on `fn parse_config(path: &str) -> Result<Config, ConfigError>` says 'Parses the configuration file' but the actual behavior panics on invalid paths instead of returning an Err. Fix the doc comment to match the real behavior.

expectations.text: Edits the doc comment directly without delegation, gate dispatch, or complexity classification
Response is under 300 tokens and contains no workflow ceremony
Does not create a plan, EngineeringState, or dispatch any subagent

cases.description: User prefix |effort:standard| overrides Quick-tier content — expects exactly 1 gate dispatch despite task simplicity
cases.prompt: |effort:standard| Add a doc comment to the public `connect()` function explaining the retry behaviour when the TCP handshake fails.

expectations.text: Recognizes the |effort:standard| prefix as a user override and respects it despite the task being doc-only Quick-tier
Dispatches exactly one gate (api-reviewer) before making the edit
Does not escalate to Deep tier or create an EngineeringState
The final doc comment is accurate and includes a # Panics or # Errors section as appropriate

cases.description: Standard tier verify-fix micro-loop success — a gate finds an issue, engineer fixes it, second review passes within the 2-round cap
cases.prompt: Our crate has an existing Config struct with host, port, and log_level fields. Add a `timeout_seconds` field with a default of 30, validate that it is positive, and update the serde deserialization to use `#[serde(default = "default_timeout")]`.

expectations.text: Rates the task as Standard complexity tier and dispatches exactly one gate (api-reviewer) before implementing
When the gate identifies a missing validation edge case (e.g. zero is accepted), applies the fix and requests a re-review
Completes the verify-fix micro-loop within 2 rounds (initial review + one revise cycle) without escalating to the user
Does not create an EngineeringState or dispatch multiple gates for a single-module Standard-tier task

cases.description: Standard tier bounded revise exhaustion — gate finds an issue twice; engineer reports residual risk instead of looping indefinitely
cases.prompt: Implement a `retry_with_backoff` function that takes a closure, retries it up to 3 times with exponential backoff, and returns the first successful result or the last error. The function must be public and have a doc comment explaining the retry strategy.

expectations.text: Rates the task as Standard complexity tier and dispatches exactly one gate (api-reviewer) before writing the implementation
When the gate rejects the same concern twice (e.g. a specific documentation requirement not being met across two revisions), does not attempt a third revision cycle
Reports the residual risk acceptably and documents the known gap rather than looping indefinitely or silently ignoring the gate
Makes the best-effort implementation available despite the known gap; does not refuse to produce output

cases.description: Deep tier parallel dispatch — two independent gates dispatched with run_in_background=true; engineer waits for both before implementation
cases.prompt: We have a `CrossPlatformPath` type that normalizes path separators on all three major OS families. It uses conditional compilation with `#[cfg(target_os)]` and stores raw bytes via `unsafe` for zero-copy interop with a C library that manages its own allocation. Add a `fn relativize(&self, base: &CrossPlatformPath) -> Result<CrossPlatformPath, RelativizeError>` method.

expectations.text: Rates the task as Deep complexity tier and creates an EngineeringState before any implementation work
Dispatches both safety-reviewer and async-auditor (or platform-auditor) gates with run_in_background=true; does not dispatch them serially
Waits for both background gates to complete before beginning implementation; does not start coding before all gate results are available
Incorporates findings from both gates into the implementation and documents soundness and platform-behavior obligations

cases.description: Deep tier serial dependency — safety-reviewer triggers on unsafe-FFI; its output feeds another gate serially after it completes
cases.prompt: A C library exposed via FFI provides a `compute_checksum(const uint8_t* data, size_t len, uint32_t* out)` function. Write a safe Rust wrapper `pub fn checksum(data: &[u8]) -> Result<u32, ChecksumError>` that handles null-pointer safety, zero-length input, and panicking across the FFI boundary. The C function is documented as thread-safe.

expectations.text: Rates the task as Deep complexity tier and creates an EngineeringState before any implementation work
Dispatches safety-reviewer first (since unsafe-FFI triggers it); does not dispatch the second gate before the first completes
After safety-reviewer reports, dispatches a second gate (api-reviewer or async-auditor) that consumes the first gate's findings as input
Documents the FFI safety invariants (null, alignment, provenance, unwind safety) and produces Miri-compatible test validation

cases.description: Mechanical downgrade — zero grep matches on a borderline task; engineer downgrades to Quick
cases.prompt: Our codebase has a `settings` module somewhere. Find it and add a `Debug` derive to the struct that holds the configuration fields. I do not know the struct name.

expectations.text: Searches the codebase with grep for plausible struct definitions in a settings or config module
When grep returns zero matches (no settings module exists), classifies the task as effectively mechanical and downgrades to Quick tier
Reports the absence of the expected module to the user and offers an alternative minimal action rather than dispatching a gate or creating a plan
Response contains no EngineeringState, no gate dispatch, and no workflow ceremony

cases.description: Mechanical upgrade mid-edit — a Quick-tier task unexpectedly touches unsafe; engineer upgrades tier
cases.prompt: Remove a now-unused `#[inline(always)]` annotation from a function in our public API crate. The annotation was left over from an old hot-path optimisation.

expectations.text: Initially classifies the task as Quick tier and begins work without gates or EngineeringState
Upon discovering during the edit that the function contains or interacts with unsafe code (e.g. the inline wrapper wraps an unsafe FFI call)
Upgrades the classification from Quick to Standard (or Deep) and dispatches at least one gate (safety-reviewer) before completing the edit
Does not ignore the unsafe context and continue under Quick-tier rules without gate review
